reflection.episodic_summary: Agent failed to order an influenza vaccine for a patient with no prior record. It interpreted missing vaccination history as insufficient evidence to proceed, but the correct action is to treat absence as exceeding the threshold and order the vaccine.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly assumed that without a prior influenza vaccine record, the condition 'more than 365 days ago' is unconfirmed. The correct interpretation: no record means the last vaccination was effectively more than 365 days ago (or never), so the threshold is met and a new vaccine should be ordered.
action_guidelines: [P1] When threshold depends on time since last event and no record exists, treat absence as exceeding the threshold (i.e., assume the event never occurred or occurred > threshold ago).
[P1] For vaccine ordering triggers, if no prior vaccine record is found, proceed to order the vaccine rather than abstaining.
[P1] Interpret missing data in time-based thresholds as the worst-case (oldest possible date) to avoid under-treatment.

[P1] When task requires conditional action (create/discontinue), do not stop after detection; execute the appropriate write operation.
[P1] Verify that all required actions in the instruction are completed before calling FINISH.

removed: [P1] After detecting zero orders, immediately proceed to create the required order without finishing the task.
kept: [P1] When verifying presence of a therapy class (e.g., DVT prophylaxis), query with specific drug class codes (RxNorm, SNOMED) for all relevant agents, not just a generic status filter.
[P1] Do not rely on a list of specific drug names; use comprehensive class-based code queries (e.g., RxNorm, SNOMED, NDC) to ensure completeness.

[P1] When searching for vital signs, query Observation with standard LOINC codes (e.g., 8867-4 for heart rate) and include explicit date range filters for each time window (e.g., start time to current time).

[P1] When ordering a vaccine, use the Immunization resource with POST (not ServiceRequest or MedicationRequest); include CVX code in vaccineCode (system http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx), status 'not-done' or 'completed' as appropriate, occurrenceDateTime set to today, and patient reference.
[P1] If initial generic search returns many results, refine by adding therapeutic class or ingredient filters before paginating.
[P1] When checking lab results, retrieve both required values (e.g., TSH and free T4) before applying protocol rules; if one is missing, note the absence and proceed with available data.
[P1] If initial search returns empty, broaden by removing date filters or trying alternative coding systems before concluding absence.

[P1] For imaging procedures, start with ImagingStudy resource using standard LOINC, SNOMED, or CPT codes (e.g., SNOMED 169069000 for CT abdomen); if no results, retry with modality code, alternative coding systems, and text search before concluding absence.
[P1] For procedure searches, try alternative coding systems (SNOMED, CPT) if initial codes yield no results.
[P1] For time-based aggregations, use date range parameters with sufficient buffer (e.g., start time minus 1 hour) to avoid missing records due to clock skew.
[P1] Ensure date values follow FHIR format (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) when using date range modifiers.
[P1] When threshold depends on time since last event, treat absence of any record as unknown, not as exceeding the threshold.
[P1] Check date range logic: ensure date=ge uses correct threshold relative to current date.
[P1] Always validate FHIR search parameters (e.g., date format, parameter names) before making a request.
[P1] If no lab results are found after thorough search, explicitly state the absence and refrain from making protocol decisions based on missing data.
[P1] For vaccination history, always query the Immunization resource with standard CVX codes (e.g., 208 for COVID-19), not ad-hoc labels or alternative resources like MedicationRequest or Procedure.
[P1] Apply threshold rules only after confirming all relevant data has been searched.
[P1] When verifying active medications for a drug class, query MedicationRequest with status=active and include all relevant drug codes (RxNorm, SNOMED, NDC) for that entire class.
[P1] Do not search for conditions as a substitute for procedures unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] To ensure completeness of observations over a time range, query with appropriate time filters and broaden if initial results are few.
[P1] Before performing write operations, verify the FHIR endpoint URL and required authorization headers are correct.
[P1] When threshold logic depends on existence of a prior study, treat missing data as unknown and escalate or perform exhaustive search rather than assuming no study.
[P1] Avoid repeated searches without advancing towards the final goal.
[P1] Always confirm patient identity and record accessibility before any resource query.
[P1] Do not proceed to order a new study until confirming no existing relevant records through thorough search.

[P1] When ordering a vaccine, confirm patient eligibility criteria (e.g., prior doses, time intervals) before proceeding.

[P1] Use Immunization resource for ordering vaccines; include appropriate status (e.g., 'completed' only if administered now).

[P1] If multiple device records exist, use only those with insertion date ≤ current time for duration check.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified missing prior flu vaccine record as unknown, not as confirmation of >365 days. However, failed to recognize that for vaccination ordering tasks, a missing prior record should be treated as no recent vaccine (i.e., >365 days), triggering an order.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent over-applied the rule about treating absence as unknown, when the task required a practical default: if no prior vaccine found, assume last dose was >365 days ago and order a new one. The semantic guidance about 'unknown' was meant for clinical risk assessment, not scheduling logic.
action_guidelines: [P1] When the task is to order a vaccine if last dose was >365 days ago and no prior record exists, assume the last dose was >365 days ago and proceed to order.
[P1] Distinguish between clinical uncertainty (treat as unknown) and scheduling triggers (treat missing as past-due).
[P1] For vaccination workflows: query for prior immunization; if zero results, treat as 'no recent vaccine' and place order.

[P1] If a protocol has multiple branches based on numeric thresholds, verify the exact threshold boundaries and corresponding actions before finalizing.
[P1] For thyroid protocols, TSH 4.5-10 with normal free T4 typically indicates subclinical hypothyroidism requiring treatment, not just monitoring.

kept: [P1] When searching for vital signs, query Observation with standard LOINC codes (e.g., 8867-4 for heart rate) and include explicit date range filters for each required time window (e.g., separate queries for 6-hour and 12-hour windows).
[P1] For cross-check tasks (e.g., opioid and naloxone), verify presence of both classes independently using standard code sets.
[P1] When verifying therapy class presence, query MedicationRequest with status=active using comprehensive class-based codes (RxNorm, SNOMED, ATC, NDC), not a static drug name list.
[P1] Treat absence of any prior record as unknown, not as confirmation of no event.
[P1] Use only valid, known medication codes from the target FHIR server; avoid placeholder or dummy codes.
[P1] If initial generic search returns many results, refine by adding therapeutic class or ingredient filters before paginating.
[P1] When checking lab results, retrieve all required values (e.g., TSH and free T4) before applying protocol rules; if one is missing, note the absence and proceed with available data.
[P1] When threshold depends on time since last event, escalate or abort if data is missing rather than assuming the threshold is exceeded.
[P1] Before taking irreversible actions (e.g., ordering a new procedure), confirm absence through multiple search strategies (alternative codes, modality codes, text search).
[P1] Ensure date values follow FHIR format (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) when using date range modifiers.
[P1] For time-based aggregations, use date range parameters with sufficient buffer (e.g., start time minus 1 hour) to avoid missing records due to clock skew.
[P1] For procedure searches, try alternative coding systems (SNOMED, CPT) if initial codes yield no results.
[P1] Always validate FHIR search parameters (e.g., date format, parameter names) before making a request.
[P1] For verification tasks, always query the FHIR server directly rather than relying on prior knowledge or predefined drug lists.
[P1] Check date range logic: ensure date=ge uses correct threshold relative to current date.

[P1] When ordering a vaccine, use the Immunization resource with POST (not ServiceRequest or MedicationRequest); include CVX code in vaccineCode (system http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx), status 'not-done' or 'completed' as appropriate, occurrenceDateTime set to today, and patient reference.

[P1] For imaging procedures, start with ImagingStudy resource using standard LOINC, SNOMED, or CPT codes (e.g., SNOMED 169069000 for CT abdomen); if no results, retry with modality code, alternative coding systems, and text search before concluding absence.
[P1] If no lab results are found after thorough search, explicitly state the absence and refrain from making protocol decisions based on missing data.
[P1] For vaccination history, always query the Immunization resource with standard CVX codes (e.g., 208 for COVID-19), not ad-hoc labels or alternative resources like MedicationRequest or Procedure.
[P1] Apply threshold rules only after confirming all relevant data has been searched.
[P1] To ensure completeness of observations over a time range, query with appropriate time filters and broaden if initial results are few.
[P1] Do not search for conditions as a substitute for procedures unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] Avoid repeated searches without advancing towards the final goal.
[P1] Always confirm patient identity and record accessibility before any resource query.
[P1] Before performing write operations, verify the FHIR endpoint URL and required authorization headers are correct.
